facts.truth: Helicity H = integral(u dot omega) is exactly conserved for smooth solutions of 3D inviscid incompressible Euler equations.
distractors: Helicity is approximately conserved but decays slowly in Euler flow.
Helicity conservation requires additional assumptions beyond incompressibility.
Helicity is only conserved in 2D Euler, not 3D.

facts.truth: Helicity is scaling-critical under the NS rescaling u_lambda = lambda u(lambda x, lambda^2 t), with scaling exponent zero.
distractors: Helicity is scaling-supercritical under NS rescaling.
Helicity is scaling-subcritical under NS rescaling.
Helicity scaling exponent is +1 (subcritical).

facts.truth: Helicity is NOT sign-definite: u perpendicular to omega everywhere gives H=0 for arbitrarily large velocity fields.
distractors: Helicity magnitude always bounds the enstrophy from below.
Helicity is always positive for incompressible flows.
Helicity is sign-definite for divergence-free vector fields.

facts.truth: The H^{1/2} Sobolev norm is NOT conserved in 3D Euler flow. Only energy and helicity are conserved.
distractors: The H^{1/2} norm is exactly conserved in 3D inviscid Euler.
The H^{1/2} norm is conserved because it equals helicity.
All Sobolev norms are conserved in inviscid Euler.

facts.truth: The L^3 norm of velocity is scaling-critical and sign-definite but NOT conserved in 3D Euler or Navier-Stokes.
distractors: The L^3 velocity norm is conserved, scaling-critical, and sign-definite.
The L^3 norm is supercritical for 3D NS.
The L^3 norm is conserved in inviscid Euler.

facts.truth: Energy (L^2 norm squared) is scaling-supercritical for 3D NS with exponent -1, meaning it grows under rescaling toward smaller scales.
distractors: Energy is scaling-critical for 3D NS.
Energy is scaling-subcritical for 3D NS.
Energy scaling exponent is zero (critical) in all dimensions.

facts.truth: CKN (1982) proves that singular points of suitable weak solutions of 3D NS have one-dimensional parabolic Hausdorff measure zero.
distractors: CKN only applies to 2D Navier-Stokes.
CKN proves full regularity for 3D NS.
CKN shows singularities must be dense in space-time.

facts.truth: The Prodi-Serrin condition for 3D NS regularity is 2/p + 3/q = 1 with q > 3, meaning u in L^p_t L^q_x implies smoothness.
distractors: The Prodi-Serrin condition requires 2/p + 3/q = 2.
The Prodi-Serrin condition only works for q less than 3.
The Prodi-Serrin condition proves unconditional regularity.

facts.truth: Tao (2016) proved finite-time blowup for an averaged version of 3D NS, showing regularity proofs must use specific nonlinear structure, not just scaling.
distractors: Tao proved regularity for averaged NS.
Tao's result applies to actual NS, not an averaged version.
Tao showed scaling alone suffices to prove regularity.

facts.truth: Geometric depletion means vortex stretching is reduced below its dimensional bound because vorticity and strain align in ways that limit effective stretching.
distractors: Geometric depletion means vorticity and strain are always perpendicular.
Geometric depletion only occurs in 2D flows.
Vortex stretching always achieves its dimensional upper bound.

facts.truth: No known quantity in 3D NS is simultaneously conserved, scaling-critical, and sign-definite. Energy is conserved but supercritical. Helicity is conserved and critical but not sign-definite.
distractors: The L^3 velocity norm has all three properties.
Energy is conserved, critical, and sign-definite in 3D.
Enstrophy has all three properties in 3D NS.
